name: AgentOps Operator Workflow Skills Canary
description: Offline public canary for high-use planning, review, investigation, refactor, optimization, PR, swarm, and provenance skill contracts that were previously only covered indirectly by aggregate skill lint.

cases.title: operator workflow skill validators pass
cases.objective: Run focused validators for high-use operator workflow skills across shared and Codex artifacts, including optional Codex validators when present.

inputs.shell: set -eu; for s in bootstrap brainstorm bug-hunt complexity converter council deps design grafana-platform-dashboard implement llm-wiki openai-docs oss-docs perf plan pr-implement pr-plan pr-prep pr-research pr-retro pr-validate readme red-team refactor retro reverse-engineer-rpi review scaffold swarm trace using-agentops; do echo "shared: $s"; bash "skills/$s/scripts/validate.sh"; done; for s in bootstrap brainstorm bug-hunt complexity converter council deps design grafana-platform-dashboard implement llm-wiki openai-docs oss-docs perf plan pr-implement pr-plan pr-prep pr-research pr-retro pr-validate readme red-team refactor retro reverse-engineer-rpi review scaffold swarm trace using-agentops; do if [ -f "skills-codex/$s/scripts/validate.sh" ]; then echo "codex: $s"; bash "skills-codex/$s/scripts/validate.sh"; else echo "codex: $s (no validator)"; fi; done

cases.title: planning and decomposition contracts include prevention context and companion-file scope
cases.objective: Protect the plan skill behavior that prevents stale planning: active findings, Applied findings lines, full touched-file inventories, Codex companion artifacts, and refresh verification.

expectations.value: Active findings from `.agents/findings/registry.jsonl` are a fallback planning input.
Every written plan must include an `Applied findings:` line

cases.title: review, investigation, red-team, and refactor skills preserve execution discipline
cases.objective: Protect human/judgment workflow contracts that decide whether a change is better or worse: findings-first review, root-cause investigation, context-restricted red-team probes, and one-step-at-a-time refactors.

expectations.value: What assumptions does this change make

cases.title: complexity, performance, and dependency workflows preserve measurable baselines
cases.objective: Ensure optimization skills remain measurement-driven: complexity reports name thresholds, performance work preserves baselines, and dependency updates validate after each change.

expectations.value: Record current state (version, lock file hash)

cases.title: documentation, wiki, OpenAI docs, and conversion skills preserve source-backed outputs
cases.objective: Protect operator workflows that turn evidence into docs or runtime artifacts without stale, speculative, or runtime-incompatible output.

expectations.value: Treat OpenAI docs as the source of truth

cases.title: open-source PR workflow skills preserve upstream fit and isolation
cases.objective: Protect contribution workflows from scope creep by checking PR research, planning, implementation, preparation, validation, and retro artifacts.

expectations.value: DO NOT PROCEED IF PRE-CHECK FAILS
Separate one-off reviewer taste from durable contribution patterns.

cases.title: swarm, codex-team, trace, and provenance skills preserve ownership and evidence chains
cases.objective: Ensure parallel execution keeps file ownership explicit and provenance tools keep source citations attached to decisions and artifacts.

expectations.value: Every TaskCreate **must** include `metadata.issue_type` plus a `metadata.files` array.
the orchestrator IS the lock manager
Every claim needs a source citation.
